queries.prompt: Returns a heterogeneous collection of the specified types for a specified team or teams. Additional filters can be passed into requested types by passing them in the url's querystring as type__filter=value (i.e. ?event__start_date=2015-01-01). Any filter can be passed that is available on the search for the specified type.

data.name: scope_to
data.prompt: A comma separated list of singular types that you would like to scope the rest of the related result sets to. For instance, scoping assignments to events and providing a filter on events will return only assignments for the events returned.

data.name: team_id
data.prompt: The team_id(s) to return results for, this can be a single id or a comma-separated list of ids.

data.name: types
data.prompt: A comma separated list of singular types that you want returned.

data.name: notes
data.value:
This is an outreach to Stowe Missions (http://stowemission.org/) that fits perfectly within our mission of providing robotics instructions for those that otherwise would not have access. 

The first night we would have 35 minutes to get approximately 20 disadvantaged 1st- 9th grade kids excited about robotics. The following three Mondays will be used to build and do activities with a Mio robot (elementary kids) and a Boe-bot (MS/HS kids). These are kits that were purchased online. If any of you have experience with them, you would be a great asset! Each student has a tutor, so there will be plenty of adult supervision. 

You do not have to attend all four nights to help out.  Please sign up to help when you can. This event can count as volunteer hours. :)

data.name: formatted_title_for_multi_team
data.value: Digital Fusion Stowe Mission Robot Demo

Just found out about an event Otterbein is hosting. They would like us to be involved in it. (Details below). We will need a team presence at The Point (Nestle space if we can still get in there) that night. This is a great opportunity to advertise the team and possibly obtain sponsors. Event information: “Otterbein will be participating in the State of the Community event with the City of Westerville, Westerville Public Library, Westerville Area Chamber of Commerce and Westerville City Schools at 6:30 p.m. on March 12 at The Point. They expect approximately 300-350 people in attendance. The program will begin at 6:30.” 

Note: It would be great to have a team brochure and the sponsor sheet to pass out at the event.
